default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: You can sit. If you like.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: I come here most days. When the weather allows.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: My son used to feed the ducks here. When he was small.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: He's not small anymore. He's not here anymore.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: That's not a sad thing. Not exactly. He's living. Somewhere else.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: I chose to let him. I choose it again every day I don't follow.

default.speaker: Ruth
default.text: Some days that feels like love. Some days it just feels like sitting by a canal.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: You don't need to say anything. Neither do I.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: He left three years ago. University, then work. Normal things.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: Everyone said: you must be proud. And I am. I am.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: But no one asked what it costs. To choose someone else's freedom over your own comfort.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: Every day I could call. Could ask him to come back. Could make him feel guilty enough.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: I don't. That's the choice. Every single day, that's the choice.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: The canal doesn't care. That's why I like it here.

existentialism.speaker: Ruth
existentialism.text: It just moves. Slowly. Wherever it's going.
